label: Finance / Banking

categories.label: Quantified Impact
categories.rubric: Score 0-30 based on quantified bullets with $, %, x, count, AUM, deal size, transaction volume, model outputs, returns, savings, or portfolio scope. Finance screens heavily for measurable outcomes: deal value, companies covered, revenue/EBITDA impact, model accuracy, variance reduction, or pitch performance. Weak: task-only bullets with no numbers.

categories.label: Finance Domain Fit
categories.rubric: Score 0-25 on relevance to finance roles. Strong: IB, PE, equity research, asset management, S&T, corporate finance, FP&A, valuation, transaction advisory, student fund, investment club, stock pitch, or finance/econ/accounting coursework. Weak: generic business interest with no finance signal.

categories.label: Technical (Finance)
categories.rubric: Score 0-15 on finance technical toolkit: Excel, financial modeling, three-statement models, DCF, comps, precedent transactions, LBO, accounting fundamentals, valuation, Bloomberg, Capital IQ, FactSet, VBA, Python/R, SQL, Power BI/Tableau. Strong bullets show built models or used tools for analysis, not just listed keywords.

categories.label: Format / ATS
categories.rubric: Score 0-10 on finance resume conventions: one page, clean sections, consistent dates, no graphics/tables that break ATS, precise role/company formatting, bullets that start with strong verbs, and no typos in firm or product names. Banking and finance screens are unforgiving on polish.

categories.label: Brand Consistency
categories.rubric: Score 0-10 on external brand and positioning: LinkedIn matches resume, finance/econ/accounting or quantitative major is clear, GPA and graduation date are present when strong, target role is obvious, and internships/clubs tell a coherent finance story.

categories.label: Extras & Leadership
categories.rubric: Score 0-10 on finance extras: investment club leadership, student-managed fund, AKPsi treasury/finance role, CFA Level I candidate/pass, stock pitch winner/finalist, published equity research, finance newsletter, relevant language, or leadership outside work with measurable responsibility.

bonus_signals: CFA Level I candidate or passed
Investment banking internship at bulge bracket, elite boutique, or middle market bank
Private equity, asset management, hedge fund, or equity research internship
Student-managed fund or investment club leadership
Stock pitch competition winner or finalist
Published equity research or investment newsletter
Bloomberg BMC or similar market-data certification
Finance-related founder, startup, or pro bono investing work
